homepage.heroSubline: Built to last in Philly's toughest conditions. We restore and strengthen historic brickwork with techniques proven against Nor'easters and freeze-thaw cycles.
aboutText: Since 2012, Legacy Masonry has been preserving Philadelphia's architectural heritage one brick at a time. Founded by third-generation masons, we understand how Philly's unique climate and aging infrastructure demand specialized solutions.

We don't cut corners. Every tuckpointing job, chimney rebuild, and foundation repair follows historic preservation standards while incorporating modern durability. Our crews train continuously on Philadelphia-specific challenges like row house settlement and salt damage.
When we're not restoring Rittenhouse Square brownstones, we're volunteering masonry skills for neighborhood playgrounds and community gardens. This city built us - we return the favor daily.

valueProps.title: Philly-Proven Methods
valueProps.description: Our mortar mixes and techniques are formulated specifically for Philadelphia's freeze-thaw cycles and humid summers.

valueProps.title: Historic Expertise
valueProps.description: We replicate original craftsmanship on pre-war homes while reinforcing structural integrity for modern demands.

valueProps.title: No Surprise Pricing
valueProps.description: Fixed bids include full material testing and engineering assessments - no hidden costs halfway through.

valueProps.title: Emergency Response
valueProps.description: 24/7 availability for storm damage repairs when your rowhouse chimney starts shedding bricks.

homepage.ctaSubline: Schedule a free masonry assessment with our lead estimator today.

services.name: Brick Tuckpointing
services.shortDescription: Precision mortar replacement that stops water infiltration in Philadelphia's historic brickwork.
services.description:
Philadelphia's freeze-thaw cycles destroy aging mortar joints faster than anywhere in the Northeast. Our tuckpointing process begins with mortar analysis to match original composition, followed by surgical removal of deteriorated sections without damaging surrounding bricks.

We use lime-based mortars that allow historic masonry to breathe while resisting salt damage from winter streets. Every joint is tooled to match existing profiles - whether it's the concave joints on Society Hill townhomes or flush finishes on industrial loft conversions. Post-work, we apply penetrating sealants only where needed to prevent trapping moisture behind facades.
process: Step 1: Mortar sampling and structural assessment
Step 2: Precision grinding of failed joints (1/4"-1/2" depth)
Step 3: Hand-packed mortar application with period-appropriate tools
Step 4: 48-hour curing protection and final inspection
benefits: Prevents costly brick spalling from water penetration
Reduces winter heating loss through gaps

faq.question: How often should Philly rowhouses be tuckpointed?
faq.answer: Most historic brick needs repointing every 30-50 years, but units near busy roads with street salt exposure may require attention every 20 years.

faq.question: Will new mortar change my brick's appearance?
faq.answer: We custom-tint mortars to blend seamlessly and can artificially weather new joints upon request for uniform aesthetics.

services.name: Chimney Rebuilding
services.shortDescription: Structurally sound chimney restorations that withstand Philly's Nor'easters.
services.description:
From Chestnut Hill stone chimneys to Fishtown's brick stacks, we rebuild Philadelphia chimneys to handle 90mph winds and ice dams. Our process starts with thermal imaging to identify hidden frost damage, followed by complete disassembly of compromised sections using historical bricklaying patterns.

We install stainless steel liners and seismic ties on all rebuilds - critical for older homes near subway lines. Flashing details follow Philadelphia Roofing Code amendments for ice belt protection. For landmark districts, we source reclaimed brick from local salvage yards to maintain period authenticity.
process: Step 1: Core sample analysis and stability testing
Step 2: Custom scaffolding setup for tight rowhouse alleys
Step 3: Brick-by-brick reconstruction with reinforced footing
Step 4: Pressure testing and crown seal application
benefits: Eliminates fire hazards from cracked flues
Prevents attic leaks during heavy rains

faq.question: Can you match my original 19th-century brick?
faq.answer: Our relationships with local reclaim yards allow us to source period-appropriate materials, including handmade bricks with original sand finishes.

faq.question: How long until I can use my fireplace?
faq.answer: New masonry requires 30 days of curing before first use. We provide temporary caps during this period.

services.name: Stone Foundation Repair
services.shortDescription: Stabilizing Philadelphia's aging fieldstone foundations against shifting soils.
services.description:
The Delaware Valley's clay-heavy soils wreak havoc on historic stone foundations. Our repair process combines traditional dry-stone techniques with modern stabilization methods. After excavating to footer depth, we install helical piers in problem areas while preserving original stonework where structurally sound.

For Victorian homes in West Philly, we replicate rubble stone patterns using locally quarried materials. All repairs include French drain systems to manage the high water table. We coordinate directly with Philadelphia L&I for permit approvals on structural modifications.
process: Step 1: Laser level assessment and soil testing
Step 2: Temporary shoring installation
Step 3: Stone resetting with lime-based grout
Step 4: Drainage system integration

faq.question: Will repairs disturb my finished basement?
faq.answer: We use mini-excavators for interior work and contain dust with industrial barriers. Most projects require just 2-3 days of interior access.

faq.question: How do you handle shared foundation walls?
faq.answer: We file party wall agreements and coordinate access with adjoining property owners per Philadelphia code requirements.

services.name: Brick Walkway Installation
services.shortDescription: Durable brick pavements designed for Philadelphia's foot traffic and winters.
services.description:
From Rittenhouse Square gardens to Northern Liberties courtyards, we build brick walkways that withstand decades of Philadelphia use. Our installations begin with 12" gravel bases and edge restraints to prevent frost heave damage. We use vitrified pavers with non-slip finishes for icy conditions.

Patterns range from traditional herringbone to contemporary running bonds, with custom options for historic districts. All joints receive polymeric sand that resists weed growth and ant colonies. We grade all paths to divert runoff from building foundations.
process: Step 1: Subsoil compaction testing
Step 2: Grading for 1/4" per foot slope
Step 3: Hand-laid brick installation
Step 4: Joint sealing and sealing
benefits: Eliminates tripping hazards from settled paths

faq.question: How thick should brick walkways be in Philly?
faq.answer: We recommend 2 3/8" thick pavers over 12" base for residential use - commercial areas require 3" thickness.

faq.question: Can you match my existing brick?
faq.answer: We maintain samples from local brickyards dating to the 1800s for seamless repairs to historic properties.

services.name: Concrete Parging
services.shortDescription: Protective coatings for Philadelphia's vulnerable foundation walls.
services.description:
Philadelphia's basement walls need defense against hydrostatic pressure and road salt. Our parging process uses polymer-modified mixes that bond permanently to existing masonry. After pressure washing to remove efflorescence, we apply two coats with reinforcing mesh at stress points.

The finished surface resists 1500psi compressive strength - crucial for rowhouses with narrow alley drainage. We texture finishes to match surrounding masonry and include vapor-permeable waterproofing for complete protection. All work comes with freeze-thaw cycle testing reports.
process: Step 1: Surface preparation with diamond grinding
Step 2: Hydraulic cement patching of major voids
Step 3: Reinforced base coat application
Step 4: Finish coat with custom tooling

faq.question: How long before parging needs replacement?
faq.answer: Our mixes typically last 15-20 years in Philadelphia conditions - twice as long as standard mixes.

faq.question: Will it work on crumbling foundations?
faq.answer: We first stabilize severely damaged walls with carbon fiber straps before parging for permanent results.

reviews.name: Marcus T.
reviews.text: Legacy saved our 1920s chimney after a Nor'easter took out the top courses. They sourced matching brick from a demolished factory in Kensington and rebuilt it with stainless steel reinforcement. Two winters later - not a single loose brick.

reviews.name: Eleanor R.
reviews.text: The team restored our Society Hill townhouse's Flemish bond brickwork perfectly. They even matched the original oyster shell mortar color by analyzing samples under our porch. Historic Commission approved the work without revisions.

reviews.name: Javier M.
reviews.text: Our Manayunk basement stopped flooding after Legacy repointed the fieldstone and installed a proper drainage system. They worked around our finished walls with minimal disruption and explained every step in detail.

reviews.name: Danielle K.
reviews.text: The new herringbone walkway handles our dog-walking traffic and Philly winters better than the old concrete ever did. Crew cleaned up every day and finished ahead of schedule despite January weather.

reviews.name: Thomas W.
reviews.text: After three failed attempts by other contractors, Legacy's parging finally stopped our basement leaks. Their custom mix includes some additive that prevents salt damage from the sidewalk. Dry all last spring during heavy rains.

reviews.name: Rebecca S.
reviews.text: Found Legacy when the mortar on our Fishtown rowhouse started falling out in chunks. They identified improper Portland cement repairs from the 80s as the culprit and fixed it right with lime mortar. No more spalling bricks above our front door.

service-areas.title: Philadelphia Masonry Service Areas
service-areas.description: Serving Philadelphia and surrounding communities with expert brick, stone, and concrete masonry services.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Masonry proudly serves Philadelphia, providing expert masonry services for both historic and modern properties. Whether you need brickwork, stonework, or concrete repairs, our skilled craftsmen are equipped to handle projects of any scale. From restoring historic landmarks to constructing new residential and commercial buildings, we bring precision and artistry to every job. Philadelphia's diverse architecture demands high-quality masonry, and we are committed to preserving and enhancing the city's unique character through our work.
serviceAreas.localContext: Philadelphia is known for its rich history, iconic brick row houses, and historic landmarks like Independence Hall.

serviceAreas.content: Legacy Masonry is a trusted name in Bensalem, offering top-tier masonry services for homes and businesses. Our team specializes in brick and stone installations, repairs, and custom designs tailored to the community's needs. Whether you're looking to enhance your property's curb appeal or need structural repairs, we deliver durable and aesthetically pleasing results. Bensalem's growing residential and commercial sectors make it a key area for our high-quality masonry services.
serviceAreas.localContext: Bensalem is a bustling suburb with a mix of residential neighborhoods and commercial developments.

serviceAreas.content: In Cherry Hill, Legacy Masonry provides exceptional masonry services to homeowners and businesses alike. Our expertise includes brickwork, stone veneer, and concrete installations that enhance both functionality and beauty. Cherry Hill's upscale neighborhoods and commercial properties benefit from our attention to detail and commitment to quality. Whether it's a new construction project or a restoration, we ensure lasting results that meet the highest standards.
serviceAreas.localContext: Cherry Hill is a thriving community known for its excellent schools, shopping centers, and well-maintained homes.
